title: How to pull and then push estargz images?
body:
### Issue Description

What is the correct way to pushing/pulling an estargz image via podman from one registry to another?

Example
```
podman pull ghcr.io/stargz-containers/golang:1.18-esgz
podman tag ghcr.io/stargz-containers/golang:1.18-esgz <local-registry>/stargz-containers/golang:1.18-esgz
podman push <local-registry>/stargz-containers/golang:1.18-esgz
```

This above won't push back an estargz image to a local registry.
I do also have stargz-store enabled and see that podman does lazy pulling working, but push still produces a non-estargz image.

I can get the behavior I want by using skopeo, but would like to just use podman directly if possible since most of our CI builds tend to use docker (with an alias to podman), so I don't want to introduce another tool in the mix.
```
skopeo copy docker://ghcr.io/stargz-containers/golang:1.18-esgz docker://<local-registry>/stargz-containers/golang:1.18-esgz
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

comments.body: we cannot push estargz images from the local storage, we miss the tooling for recreating them and even if we supported it, we could still not regenerate the exact same blob since the compression could be different.  The safest, as mentioned, is to use `skopeo` and move the original blobs
